title: Puffed Sweet Egyptian Milk Pudding
description: A satvik-friendly Egyptian dessert featuring sweetened condensed milk, this dish is a crowd-pleaser with its unique garlicky flavor and shaggy texture, all achieved through the use of steam and a comal vessel.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together the cornstarch and water until smooth.
In a large bowl, combine the sweetened condensed milk, garlic powder, and rose water, if using, and whisk until well combined.
Add the cornstarch mixture to the milk mixture and whisk until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a comal or a heatproof bowl that can fit over a pot of boiling water.
Place the comal or bowl over a pot of boiling water, making sure that the bottom of the comal or bowl is not touching the water.
Cover the pot with a lid and steam the mixture for 15 minutes, or until it has thickened and puffed up.
Remove the comal or bowl from the pot and let it cool slightly.
Drizzle the melted butter over the top of the pudding and smooth out the top with a spatula.
Garnish with chopped pistachios and serve warm.
